reasoning: All three nail the core reasoning: observational design, confounding, reverse causation, relative-vs-absolute denominator, and the whole-coffee-vs-extract substitution leap, with no causal overclaiming. B wins decisively on calibration and depth: it assigns explicit posterior probabilities (20-25% causal, <10% supplement, 75-80% the headline is overstated), invokes an outside-view base rate grounded in the vitamin E / beta-carotene / HRT replication graveyard, raises multiple comparisons, and names Mendelian randomization as discriminating evidence. C is strong and uniquely grounds itself in real cited literature (the 2023 review's ~4% per cup vs the brand's 20%, plus FDA disease-claim rules), which adds real-world usefulness, but it never quantifies its belief and its citations are unverifiable in a blind setting. A is clean, well-structured, and rhetorically sharp ('precise estimate of a biased number') but stays qualitative and offers the least beyond the standard checklist, so it ranks third.
